message:
fix(core): avoid duplicate trigger buffer accumulation when compression is enabled

When compression is enabled, the uncompressed_buffer already holds
the raw bytes needed for the definitive trigger scan at flush time.
Previously, trigger_data_buffer also accumulated the same bytes,
doubling memory usage.

Now process_chunk() only writes to trigger_data_buffer when
compression is disabled.  New definitive_trigger_bytes() helper
selects the correct source at flush time.

should_flush() checks whichever buffer is active for the current
config instead of always checking both.

Tests:
- chunked_pipeline_reuses_compression_buffer_for_trigger_flush:
  verifies trigger_data_buffer stays empty when compression on.
- chunked_pipeline_keeps_trigger_buffer_when_compression_disabled:
  verifies trigger_data_buffer used when compression off.
- Proptest additions for scan pipeline coverage.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
